items.blurb: CJ McCollum's Late Fadeaway Lifts Hawks Over Knicks 109-108 To Take Game 3 — CJ McCollum converted a 15-foot fadeaway jumper with 12.5 seconds remaining Thursday night, giving the Atlanta Hawks a 109-108 victory over the New York Knicks in Game 3. items.headline: What did Scottie Barnes and the Raptors prove in Game 3?
items.blurb: What did Scottie Barnes and the Raptors prove in Game 3? — Michael Grange joins Sportsnet Central to discuss Scottie Barnes’ dominant performance in the Toronto Raptors’ Game 3 win over the Cleveland Cavaliers, while highlighting the stellar recent play from RJ Barrett and rookie Collin Murray-Boyles.

items.headline: ‘I’m a dog’: Raptors’ Barrett after 33-point performance in Game 3
items.blurb: ‘I’m a dog’: Raptors’ Barrett after 33-point performance in Game 3 — Toronto Raptors forward RJ Barrett joins Danielle Michaud after the team’s 126-104 over the Cleveland Cavaliers in Game 3, saying he’s “a dog” after a 33-point performance and the group won the game “for Toronto.”

items.blurb: Barrett delivers ‘unbelievable’ performance at home as Raptors get back in series — So many different Raptors had memorable nights in Game 3, you could make a list. But in some ways, this game belonged to RJ Barrett.

Following an investigation including multiple interviews and video review, the league found no basis to any claim of bias or misconduct by game officials. items.headline: Austin Reaves Upgraded To Questionable For Game 3
items.blurb: Austin Reaves Upgraded To Questionable For Game 3 — The Los Angeles Lakers have upgraded Austin Reaves to questionable for Game 3 of their first round series against the Houston Rockets as he works his way back from a Grade 2 left oblique strain. items.blurb: Aaron Gordon Misses Game 3 With Calf Injury — Aaron Gordon was held out by the Denver Nuggets for Game 3 on Thursday with calf tightness. Gordon was listed as probable initially on the injury report, but was downgraded to questionable on Thursday morning and then officially ruled out two hours before the start of the game. items.blurb: Bucks to hire Taylor Jenkins as head coach — The Milwaukee Bucks are finalizing a contract agreement with Taylor Jenkins and will hire him as their new head coach, sources tell ESPN’s Shams Charania and Ramona Shelburne.

items.blurb: Joel Embiid Upgraded To Doubtful For Game 3 — Joel Embiid has been upgraded to doubtful by the Philadelphia 76ers ahead of Game 3 on Friday against the Boston Celtics. Embiid underwent emergency appendectomy two weeks ago. Embiid began a strength and conditioning program Monday and then participated in parts of practice Thursday. items.blurb: Giannis Antetokounmpo Hasn't Communicated With Bucks' Upper Brass Since Before Deadline — Giannis Antetokounmpo has not had any substantive dialogue with the Milwaukee Bucks' upper brass since the trade deadline, sources tell ESPN. items.blurb: Bucks To Hire Taylor Jenkins As Head Coach — Taylor Jenkins is nearing agreement to become the next head coach of the Milwaukee Bucks. Jenkins was an assistant coach with the Bucks during the 2018-19 season under Mike Budenholzer before becoming head coach of the Memphis Grizzlies. items.blurb: What factors will decide the Lakers-Rockets playoff series? — When the Los Angeles Lakers' two leading scorers, Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves, went down against the Oklahoma City Thunder on April 2, many speculated their playoff hopes were diminished and that a short series was inevitable. Instead, they're up 2-0 on the Rockets. What will determine how the rest of the series plays out?

items.blurb: Derrick White Wins 2025-26 NBA Sportsmanship Award — Boston Celtics guard Derrick White has been selected as the 2025-26 NBA Sportsmanship Award recipient . White claims the Joe Dumars Trophy for the first time in his nine-year career. items.headline: Steve Kerr Decision Hasn't Been Made As Internal Optimism Wanes
items.blurb: Steve Kerr Decision Hasn't Been Made As Internal Optimism Wanes — Optimism within the Golden State Warriors organization about Steve Kerr's return has dimmed in recent weeks, multiple team sources told The Athletic. items.headline: AJ Dybantsa Declares For 2026 NBA Draft
items.blurb: AJ Dybantsa Declares For 2026 NBA Draft — AJ Dybantsa has declared for the 2026 NBA Draft, leaving BYU after his freshman season. Dybantsa is one of the leading candidates to be selected with the No. 1 overall pick.
